title: Wastewater grab sample processing with Nanotrap® Microbiome A particles (40 ml)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"5pr8b","text":"This protocol is used to carry out processing of wastewater using Nanotrap‱ Microbiome A Particles (Ceres Nanosciences). Nanotrap‱particles are affinity-capture magnetic hydrogel particles that have been shown to capture and concentrate microorganisms (like SARS-CoV-2) from wastewater prior to RNA or TNA extraction. The sample is mixed with the particles, incubated, and separated with a magnet. The supernatant is removed, the pellet is spiked with extraction controls, and subjected to lysis followed by extraction. This protocol has been adapted from the online protocol available from Ceres Nanosciences.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":74,"length":1},{"key":1,"offset":129,"length":1}],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"emoji","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"7C88913EC4D111EE854F0A58A9FEAC02","name":":registered:"}},"1":{"type":"emoji","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"7C88913EC4D111EE854F0A58A9FEAC02","name":":registered:"}}}}

steps: Wastewater grab samples collected in 50 mL centrifuge tubes are transported to the laboratory in cold chain, maintaining a temperature of 4 °C .
Once received, place the sample tubes on a tube rack and leave at Room temperature for 10 minutes, allowing large aggregates to settle at the bottom of the tube.
Using a serological pipette, carefully transfer 40 mL of clear supernatant into a new 50 ml centrifuge tube without disturbing the sediment.
Add 400 µL of ER2 Enhancer reagent to the supernatant, invert 2-3 times and mix throughly.
Add 600 µL of Nanotrap‱ A particles to the above and invert mix 2-3 times. Incubate at Room temperature for 00:20:00 .
Place the tube on a 50 ml magnetic stand for 00:10:00 min and allow the Nanotrap® particles to settle.
Carefully discard the supernatant without disturbing the pellet. If needed, use a pipette to remove all the supernatant from the tube.
Remove the tube containing pellet from the magnetic rack. The pellet is now ready for total nucleic acid extraction. The pellet should not be frozen, extraction is to be carried out on the same day.
Add 700 µL of the InhibitEX buffer along with 1 µL of 1/10 diluted SPC and 1 µL of 1/10 diluted RNA-MS2 control into each particle pellet and resuspend the pellet by vortexing the tube briefly.
Transfer the suspension into a 1.7 mL microcentrifuge tube. Incubate at Room temperature for 00:10:00 .
Place the microcentrifuge tubes on a 1.5 ml magnetic separation rack until the Nanotrap® particles have settled, resulting in a clear solution.
Collect the supernatant into 2 mL microcentrifuge tube containing ~ 370 mg of acid-washed glass beads and discard the pellet.
creator: Dilip Abraham
